question_title: Which team will win the 2026 Six Nations Championship?
summary: France leads the 2026 Six Nations after 3 of 5 rounds with a perfect 15 points (3 wins, 0 losses) and a dominant +89 point differential. They hold a 4-point lead over Scotland (11 pts) and 6-point lead over Ireland (9 pts). France only needs to win 1 of their 2 remaining matches (Scotland away R4, England at home R5) to effectively clinch the title. My Monte Carlo simulation (100k iterations) gives France ~90% win probability, consistent with but slightly below bookmaker odds of 93-96%. Scotland is the only realistic challenger at ~6%, requiring them to beat France at Murrayfield AND win all remaining games while France also loses to England. Ireland at ~3% needs an even more improbable chain of results. England, Italy, and Wales are effectively eliminated.

factors.description: France leads with 15 pts after 3/5 rounds, 4-point lead over Scotland (11 pts) - dominant tournament position
France has +89 point differential (next best Scotland +11), indicating massive dominance in match quality
Betting odds (Betfair 1/14 ~93%, Ladbrokes 1/25 ~96%) overwhelmingly favor France after R3
France only needs to win 1 of 2 remaining games; P(win at least 1) ≈ 92% given they face Scotland away and England at home
Even if France loses both remaining games, they'd still have 15 pts - rivals must accumulate ~16+ which requires winning both remaining games with bonus points
Scotland have Murrayfield home advantage vs France in R4 - their best and possibly only chance to close the gap
Scotland still needs to beat Ireland away in R5 AND have France lose to England - compound probability makes this unlikely
Ireland need France to lose both remaining games AND must win both their own matches - extremely unlikely compound event chain
England (5 pts), Italy (5 pts), Wales (1 pt) cannot realistically close the 10+ point gap to France in 2 rounds

reasoning:
**2026 Six Nations Championship Forecast**

After 3 of 5 rounds, France dominates with 15 points (3 wins, +89 point differential), holding a commanding 4-point lead over Scotland (11 pts) and 6-point lead over Ireland (9 pts).

**Key findings:**
- France needs just 1 win from their remaining 2 matches (Scotland away, England at home) to lock the title
- Monte Carlo simulation (100k iterations) → **France 90%**, Scotland 6%, Ireland 3%
- Bookmaker consensus (Betfair/Ladbrokes) is slightly more bullish on France at 93-96%
- Scotland's only realistic path: beat France at Murrayfield + beat Ireland in Dublin + France lose to England — a ~6% compound probability
- England, Italy, and Wales are effectively eliminated

| Team | Probability |
|------|------------|
| France | 90.0% |
| Scotland | 6.0% |
| Ireland | 3.0% |
| England | 0.5% |
| Italy | 0.3% |
| Wales | 0.2% |

resolution_reason: The 2026 Six Nations Championship has concluded. France won the tournament, securing their second successive title by defeating England 48-46 in the final match on March 14, 2026. Thomas Ramos scored a last-minute penalty to clinch the victory. This is confirmed by multiple credible sources including Wikipedia, the official Six Nations website (sixnationsrugby.com), Sky Sports, and ESPN. France finished first in the table, edging out Ireland (who won the Triple Crown) by two table points.
resolution_criteria: This question resolves to the option corresponding to the team ranked first in the final 2026 Six Nations table as shown on the official [2026 Six Nations Championship](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Six_Nations_Championship). If no champion is shown as the table leader before May 1, 2026 due to cancellation or an incomplete competition, this question will be annulled. The primary source is the official Six Nations table page: https://www.sixnationsrugby.com/en/m6n/fixtures/202600/table
fine_print: This question resolves strictly using the final table order shown on the official table page, including any tie-breakers embedded in that table. If the official table page is unavailable, resolve using credible sources per https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions and prefer sources that reproduce the official final standings. Candidate Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Six_Nations_Championship
background_info:
This forecasts the overall champion of the 2026 Six Nations, a major annual international rugby competition.
